steps.title: Apply function composition
steps.content:
Since $h(x) = f(x-4)$, substitute $(x-4)$ for $x$ in the definition of $f(x) = 304 \cdot 2^x$:
$$h(x) = f(x-4) = 304 \cdot 2^{x-4}$$

steps.title: Use exponent rules to separate the terms
steps.content:
Rewrite $2^{x-4}$ using the rule $a^{m-n} = a^m \cdot a^{-n}$:
$$2^{x-4} = 2^x \cdot 2^{-4} = 2^x \cdot \frac{1}{16}$$
Substitute back into the expression for $h(x)$:
$$h(x) = 304 \cdot 2^x \cdot \frac{1}{16} = \frac{304}{16} \cdot 2^x$$

steps.title: Evaluate the coefficient
steps.content:
Divide to find the constant factor:
$$\frac{304}{16} = 19$$
Therefore $h(x) = 19 \cdot 2^x$. That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
